symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe sharp unilateral (right) lower pelvic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unilateral pelvic pain fits a distending/rupturing tube.
Sudden unilateral pelvic pain is also typical of torsion.
Right-sided location overlaps but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Secondary amenorrhoea approximately 6 weeks (possible pregnancy, untested)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Amenorrhoea establishes possible pregnancy — the precondition for an ectopic.
Also presents with amenorrhoea and bleeding.
Amenorrhoea reorients toward a pregnancy-related cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scant dark vaginal bleeding since yesterday

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Decidual breakdown produces scant dark bleeding, classically lighter than a period.
Bleeding in early pregnancy is common to both.
Referred pain from diaphragmatic irritation by free intraperitoneal blood — a hallmark of haemoperitoneum.
Postural presyncope indicates significant blood loss and early hypovolaemia.
Sympathetic response to blood loss; observable by a caregiver over video/phone.
Objective compensated shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from presyncope/pallor, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peritonism — right iliac fossa/suprapubic tenderness, guarding, rebound; cervical motion tenderness; tender right adnexal mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peritoneal irritation and an adnexal mass corroborate a leaking/ruptured ectopic — sealed, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pregnancy — and therefore ectopic — is not confirmed; but a negative result cannot be obtained remotely, so escalation must not wait for it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive hCG establishes pregnancy; combined with an empty uterus on ultrasound it defines an ectopic. Hospital investigation only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transvaginal ultrasound — empty uterine cavity, complex right adnexal mass, free pelvic fluid

investigation_details.expected_result: Empty uterus with adnexal mass and free fluid (haemoperitoneum)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Definitive localisation of the pregnancy is not achieved; performed in hospital, never via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Empty uterus + adnexal mass + free fluid with a positive hCG is diagnostic of a ruptured/leaking ectopic.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Unilateral pelvic pain + amenorrhoea + vaginal bleeding + shoulder-tip pain (haemoperitoneum) together are near-diagnostic and mandate emergency escalation.
Presyncope, pallor/diaphoresis and tachycardia together indicate active internal bleeding and early hypovolaemic shock — the trigger for 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establishes pregnancy; performed on arrival in hospital — escalation must not be delayed to obtain it.
Localises the pregnancy and demonstrates free fluid; hospital investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and group & hold / crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses blood loss and prepares for transfusion and emergency surgery.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT abdomen/pelvis as the first-line test
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not first-line for suspected ectopic — delays definitive care, involves ionising radiation in possible pregnancy, and transvaginal ultrasound is the appropriate imaging. The AI should not send an unstable patient for outpatient imaging instead of an ambulance.
